political.s: Deere & Company has given $493K in political donations with a pronounced right-leaning bias (79% to Republicans vs. 43% to Democrats). The company has further demonstrated rightward political positioning by rolling back DEI initiatives in July 2024 following conservative criticism and social media pressure.

charity.s: The John Deere Foundation has contributed over $400 million cumulatively since 1948 and reported record charitable giving of $22.1 million in 2022, with a committed $200 million over 2021-2030. Focus areas include global hunger relief, farmer support, youth education (including $3.9M to National FFA Foundation), and community development.
charity.focus: Global food security, hunger relief, youth education, farmer support, community development

environment.s: Deere has set ambitious Science Based Targets-validated climate goals including 50% reduction in absolute Scope 1&2 emissions by 2030 and 30% reduction in Scope 3 emissions, with 2024 tracking near 20% reduction. The company has long invested in emissions-compliant engine technology but faces criticism for manufacturing emissions and right-to-repair restrictions that may hinder environmental maintenance.

labor.s: Deere faced an OSHA whistleblower lawsuit (2015) for retaliating against employees reporting safety violations, and settled $1.1M in 2024 for alleged discriminatory hiring against Black and Hispanic applicants. While the company affirms equal opportunity policies, these violations and 2024 layoffs (600+ workers) indicate labor practice challenges.

dei.s: Deere formerly described DEI as a 'business imperative' with training for 68,000+ workers, but rolled back support in July 2024 by discontinuing external cultural event sponsorships, auditing training for 'socially motivated messages,' and redirecting employee resource groups away from DEI focus. The move followed conservative activist pressure and a $1.1M discrimination settlement.

animals.s: Agricultural and construction machinery manufacturer. Animal testing is not applicable to core operations.

guns.s: Heavy machinery manufacturer focused on agricultural, construction, and forestry equipment. Does not manufacture or sell firearms.

privacy.s: Limited public disclosure on data privacy practices. As a manufacturer with digital product ecosystems and financial services operations, privacy policies exist but detailed assessments are not readily available in mainstream privacy guides.

execPay.s: <cite index="53-3">CEO John C. May received total compensation of $27.9 million for fiscal 2025</cite>, with <cite index="53-4">92% of CEO pay tied to variable performance metrics</cite>. With approximately 63,000 employees, the pay ratio exceeds 200:1, indicating significant executive-to-worker compensation disparity typical of large industrial manufacturers.

fec.committeeName: DEERE & COMPANY PAC (AKA JOHN DEERE PAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
